candidate_answer: The text indicates that often the seller of goods will have the borrower obtain a commercial letter of credit to ensure payment; simultaneously, the buyer will have the seller obtain a standby letter of credit to ensure that the goods are delivered when agreed and in accept- able condition.
doc_title: the Federal Reserve Commercial Bank Examination Manual
evidence_span: Commercial letters of credit involving imports must be considered unsecured until the goods have passed customs, the security documents speciﬁed in the letter of credit have been pre- sented, and the goods have been veriﬁed and controlled.
